subject: Project Management
title: The Human Genome Project
description: A 60-second lesson on The Human Genome Project.

hook.text: In 1990, scientists set out to read all 3 billion letters of human DNA. It seemed impossible.

buildup.text: 20 institutions across 6 countries divided the genome into sections and sequenced in parallel.

discovery.text: Humans have about 20,000 genes — far fewer than expected. A rice plant has more.

twist.text: A private company raced the public project. Competition accelerated everything.

climax.text: Completed in 2003, the Human Genome Project launched modern genomics and personalized medicine.

punchline.text: Reading ourselves took 13 years. Understanding will take centuries.

quiz.question: What surprise did the Human Genome Project reveal?
options: Humans have far fewer genes than expected
Human DNA is identical to chimps
